source.definition: A period of twelve months or 365 days, used to measure time.

explanation.sourceText: The English word "year" originates from the Old English "ġēar", which comes from the Proto-Germanic *jēran, meaning 'year'.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „year” provine din engleza veche „ġēar”, care la rândul său derivă din proto-germanicul *jēran, cu sensul de „an”.

target.definition: Perioada de timp de douăsprezece luni sau 365 de zile.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word "an" derives directly from the Latin 'annus', meaning 'year'.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „an” provine direct din latinul 'annus', care înseamnă 'an'.

examples.sourceText: Last year I was in Romania.
examples.targetText: Anul trecut am fost în România.

learningTips.text: Remember that the Romanian word 'an' means 'year' and sounds like the English word 'on', but they have very different meanings.
The English word 'year' and Romanian 'an' both come from Latin roots, so they share a deep historical connection.
Think of 'an' as short and simple, just like the English word 'an' used before vowels, to help you recall it easily.
Link 'an' to the Latin 'annus' to remember its origin and meaning as a time period.
